message:
fix(fcp-host): enforce principal ownership on operation cancellation (br-jdaro)

Closes flywheel_connectors-jdaro (P1 auth bypass).

Reviewer found that /rpc/cancel and /rpc/operations/cancel accepted any
caller-supplied operation_id and cancelled it without any auth check:

  crates/fcp-host/src/bin/fcp-host.rs:2693-2695 — routes exposed on the
  public (non-admin-gated) router.
  crates/fcp-host/src/bin/fcp-host.rs:4208-4226 — cancel_handler takes
  only `Json<CancellationRequest>`; no bearer, zone, principal, approval,
  or capability material.
  crates/fcp-host/src/bin/fcp-host.rs:4303 — tracks caller-supplied
  InvokeRequest.id as the cancellation key.

Impact: cross-principal DoS against in-flight operations. Request IDs
are client-controlled (not host-issued secrets) so a second caller that
knows/guesses the ID can abort another caller's operation.

Fix: bind cancellation to the same principal context invoke_handler
already threads through `X-Principal` (commit 746a1e38 pattern).

Three changes:

1. `TrackedOperation` gains `owner: Option<String>`. `None` preserves
   legacy untracked-owner semantics (any caller may cancel) so
   existing unauthenticated integration tests keep passing; when
   `Some(p)`, `cancel` must be called with `asserted_principal ==
   Some(p)` or the request is rejected.

2. `CancellationController::cancel` grows an
   `asserted_principal: Option<&str>` parameter. The mismatch path
   returns `HostError::PreflightFailed`, which `map_host_error` maps
   to HTTP 403 Forbidden. Ownership check runs BEFORE any state
   mutation — a mismatched cancel cannot flip `cancel_requested` on
   another principal's operation.

3. `invoke_handler` now calls `track_with_owner(&operation_id,
   asserted_principal.as_deref())` so every invoke that carries an
   X-Principal header establishes an ownership fence.
   `cancel_handler` extracts X-Principal via the shared
   `extract_principal_header` helper and passes it to
   `CancellationController::cancel`.

Two regression tests pin the invariant:

- `cancel_rejects_mismatched_principal_when_owner_recorded` — tracks
  with owner=alice, asserts mallory and unauthenticated are both
  rejected with PreflightFailed, state untouched, original owner can
  still cancel successfully.
- `cancel_allows_any_principal_when_no_owner_recorded` — pins the
  legacy backward-compat branch so unauth'd flows aren't broken.

Legacy call sites: all 49+ inline unit-test `ctrl.cancel(...)` calls
in cancellation.rs updated to pass `None` for `asserted_principal`;
behavior identical (no owner recorded, any principal accepted). No
production caller outside invoke_handler touches these APIs.

Verified: cargo test -p fcp-host --lib cancellation — 175/175 pass
(+2 new regressions). Full --lib: 35 FAILED lines vs. 35 on baseline
(identical; all in supply_chain + admin_state + enforcement, all
pre-existing).

Cc_1 / SunnyMoose IMPLEMENTER

files.patch:
@@ -4207,13 +4207,21 @@ async fn simulate_handler(
 
 async fn cancel_handler(
     State(state): State<Arc<AppState>>,
+    headers: HeaderMap,
     Json(request): Json<CancellationRequest>,
 ) -> Result<Json<CancellationResponse>, (StatusCode, String)> {
     let operation_id = request.operation_id.clone();
+    // br-jdaro: extract the asserted principal from the same header
+    // invoke_handler writes (`X-Principal`). When the tracked operation
+    // has an owner, CancellationController::cancel enforces match and
+    // returns HostError::PreflightFailed (→ 403) on mismatch. Operations
+    // tracked without an owner stay permissive for backward compat.
+    let asserted_principal = extract_principal_header(&headers);
     let started_at = Instant::now();
     tracing::debug!(
         event = "cancel_request",
         operation_id = %operation_id,
+        asserted_principal = asserted_principal.as_deref(),
         reason = %request.reason.label(),
         cleanup = ?request.cleanup,
         return_partial = request.return_partial,
@@ -4222,7 +4230,7 @@ async fn cancel_handler(
 
     let response = state
         .cancellation
-        .cancel(&request, Utc::now())
+        .cancel(&request, asserted_principal.as_deref(), Utc::now())
         .map_err(map_host_error)?;
 
     tracing::info!(
@@ -4300,7 +4308,13 @@ async fn invoke_handler(
         return Err(map_host_error(HostError::PreflightFailed(reason)));
     }
 
-    state.cancellation.track(&operation_id);
+    // br-jdaro: record the asserted principal as the operation owner so
+    // cancel_handler can authorize cancellation. When asserted_principal
+    // is None (no X-Principal header), the operation is tracked
+    // owner-less — any caller may cancel, matching legacy behavior.
+    state
+        .cancellation
+        .track_with_owner(&operation_id, asserted_principal.as_deref());
     let invoke_result = state.registry.invoke(request).await;
     state.cancellation.complete(&operation_id);
